title: Add metrics crates and expose GET /metrics endpoint
description:
## What
Integrate the `metrics` and `metrics-exporter-prometheus` crates; expose a Prometheus scrape endpoint; register all required metrics.

## Why
Operators can scrape and alert on server health without parsing logs.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read metrics-exporter-prometheus docs and examples; identify correct macro usage (`counter!`, `gauge!`, `histogram!`); determine how to run a separate TCP listener in axum; review Prometheus data model spec for label naming rules (snake_case, no dots).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add `metrics` and `metrics-exporter-prometheus` to Cargo.toml; add `[monitoring]` config section with `metrics_enabled` (bool, default false) and `metrics_addr` (String, default "127.0.0.1:9090"); on startup when enabled, spawn a separate axum listener on metrics_addr serving GET /metrics via PrometheusHandle; register these metrics: `http_requests_total{method,path,status}` counter, `http_request_duration_seconds{method,path}` histogram, `delivery_queue_depth` gauge (pending rows), `delivery_queue_dead` gauge (dead rows), `active_websocket_connections` gauge, `active_sse_connections` gauge, `sqlite_query_duration_seconds` histogram.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration test that starts server with metrics enabled, fires HTTP requests, scrapes /metrics, parses the text exposition format, asserts correct counter values; use `promtool check metrics` as an independent oracle for format validity.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: Prometheus data model spec for correct label naming conventions; validate scraped output with `promtool check metrics` (exit 0 = valid).
- Test approach: fire N known requests, scrape /metrics, assert `http_requests_total` incremented by N; assert histogram bucket lines are present; assert all registered gauge names appear.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Do not expose metrics that leak user content, usernames, or counts of private posts
- /metrics must only be reachable on the separate metrics_addr listener, never on the public port
- metrics_enabled defaults to false so production installs are not accidentally exposed
- Label values for `path` must be route templates (e.g. `/api/v1/statuses/:id`), not raw URLs, to prevent high-cardinality label explosion
